channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to force Google to sell parts of its business in order to eliminate its online search monopoly.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5080 might get speedier video RAM than we expected – but doubts remain around this GPU
item.description: Ready to write off the RTX 5080? Not so fast, new VRAM rumor suggests Nvidia may be planning something unexpected
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Así funciona la inteligencia de precios en la red de estaciones de servicio de Cepsa
item.description: En Cepsa creen firmemente que la energía es una gran fuente de progreso para la sociedad; por este motivo, y haciendo gala de responsabilidad social corporativa, la petrolera española apuesta por la excelencia y la mejora continua.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Here’s what the Google Play lawsuit ruling means for Android users
item.description: Epic Games scored a massive victory against Google Monday, when the judge overseeing the case issued his final ruling, ordering the company to largely open up its Google Play app store to competition for the next three years.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Furious officials say misinformation is harming hurricane response
item.description: The aftermath of Hurricane Helene has sparked a flood of misinformation online, with federal, state and local leaders sounding the alarm that it is complicating an already difficult recovery process in the South.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court’s Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a statement posted on the court’s website.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Adobe is debuting a new web app for creator attribution and protection
item.description: Adobe announced a free web app for creators to apply credentials to their work, in an effort to help protect content from unauthorized use and ensure creators receive proper attribution.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Red Sea cable cuts disrupted up to 70% of traffic, says RETN
item.description: Independent global network services provider RETN says its network diagnostics show that the February 2024 cable cuts in the Red Sea, initially reported to have affected 25% of data traffic between Europe and Asia, caused far more disruption than originally estimated.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Uber announces new climate-focused updates
item.description: Uber announced a slew of climate-focused updates on Tuesday at its annual climate product release event in London, including an ability to set preferences for electric vehicles, an AI assistant to answer drivers’ questions about going electric, and a paid mentorship program.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The genAI opportunity: From ‘data to insight’ to ‘context to action’
item.description: By Bryan Kirschner, Vice President, Strategy at DataStax Data scientists have long struggled with silos and cycle time.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: San Jose Office Building Seized by Lender Amid Market Slump
item.description: Commercial property values in Bay Area cities face a widening challenge as office buildings struggle to attract tenants and retain value.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Nvidia Defends Sustainability Commitment Amid AI Energy Concerns
item.description: Nvidia defends its commitment to sustainability as concerns over AI energy consumption rise. The company reports significant reductions in energy use for training and token generation on its GPUs, citing a 2,000X reduction in energy use and a 100,000X energy reduction over the last decade.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk, Tucker Carlson laugh about post discussing hypothetical Harris assassination
item.description: Conservative media pundit Tucker Carlson hosted tech mogul Elon Musk for an interview on his online video show this week, a sit-down that began with the two laughing about Musk's support for former President Trump and what he says was a "joke" he made about assassination attempts on political figure
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Aryaka brings CASB into unified SASE fold
item.description: Aryaka continues to build out its SASE platform, adding new capabilities aimed at enhancing security and simplifying the adoption process for enterprises that want to consolidate their network and security deployments. At the heart of this update is Aryaka’s new cloud access security broker (CASB).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Día Rosa Tecnológico: el movimiento mundial de TI del CIO de Estée Lauder para financiar la investigación del cáncer de mama
item.description: Cuando se unió a The Estée Lauder Companies en 2017, Michael Smith reconoció una forma en que podría ayudar a marcar la diferencia de una manera diferente a sus típicas tareas de CIO: crear conciencia sobre el cáncer de mama.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FTC antitrust case against Amazon moves forward, several state claims dismissed
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust case against Amazon will move forward, but several state claims against the e-commerce giant were dismissed, according to a newly unsealed ruling. In a ruling filed last week under seal, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5
